title: The One with the Candy Hearts

jokes.text: She barely knows me. We just live in the same building. - Any contact? - She lent me an egg once. You're in.
jokes.setup: Ross explains minimal contact with his crush neighbor
jokes.punchline: Joey declares borrowing an egg means 'you're in'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - takes the most mundane interaction and declares romantic victory. Economy and character voice are strong.

jokes.text: Ross's nervous chuckle after greeting
jokes.setup: Ross tries to be casual greeting his crush
jokes.punchline: His obvious nervousness undermines any coolness
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward character moment. Gets the job done but not memorable.

jokes.text: Here's your egg back. I'm returning your egg.
jokes.setup: Joey devises a plan to help Ross talk to his crush
jokes.punchline: The 'smooth' line is hilariously awkward and literal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that makes the egg borrowing even funnier. Pure Joey wisdom delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: I think it's winning. - I think it's insane. - She'll love it.
jokes.setup: Friends debate the egg return strategy
jokes.punchline: Joey's delusional confidence vs. Chandler's sanity
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics. Joey's optimism vs. Chandler's realism is well-established.

jokes.text: Think it'll work? - It's suicide. The man's got an egg.
jokes.setup: Asking about Ross's chances with the egg plan
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan assessment of the absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Chandler's perfect deadpan delivery. 'The man's got an egg' is beautifully absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: You can go out with a creep any night of the year. I know I do.
jokes.setup: Advising against dating creeps on Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Chandler admits he dates creeps regularly
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler self-deprecation. Good structure but somewhat predictable for his character.

jokes.text: Pathetic mess. I know, but... Come on, man, she's needy, she's vulnerable. I'm thinking: [MIMICS CASH REGISTER]
jokes.setup: Joey describes his blind date as pathetic and needy
jokes.punchline: He sees vulnerability as an opportunity (cash register sound)
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's shallow opportunism but feels a bit mean-spirited. Character-appropriate but not elevated.

jokes.text: How do I look? - Oh, um... I don't care.
jokes.setup: Joey asks for appearance approval before his date
jokes.punchline: Chandler's blunt, uncaring response
jokes.explanation: Efficient Chandler deadpan. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Oh... my... God.
jokes.setup: Chandler realizes Joey set him up with his ex Janice
jokes.punchline: Chandler's horrified realization delivered in Janice's signature cadence
jokes.explanation: Recognition moment for fans familiar with Janice's catchphrase. More setup than punchline but effective.

jokes.text: Okay, I'm making a break for it. I'm going out the window.
jokes.setup: Chandler's panic about being set up with Janice
jokes.punchline: His extreme escape plan of jumping out a window
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Chandler's panic. Physical comedy element works for his desperation.

jokes.text: I can't go when I'm nervous.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to calm down in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: TMI about his bathroom anxiety
jokes.explanation: Bathroom humor that feels slightly forced. Gets a reaction but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: [MIMICS SOBBING] 'Oh, was it good for you?'
jokes.setup: Describing Pete the Weeper who cried during sex
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's exaggerated imitation of his crying
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's theatrical delivery makes this memorable. Good visual comedy with the crying imitation.

jokes.text: 'I win. I win.' I went out with him for two months. I didn't get to win once.
jokes.setup: Monica describes Howard the 'I win' guy
jokes.punchline: Her competitive frustration at never winning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica - her competitiveness extends even to dating. Character-specific and relatable frustration.

jokes.text: I think we're like some kind of magnets. I know I am. That's why I can't wear a digital watch.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they attract bad boyfriends
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's literal interpretation involving digital watches
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe logic - takes metaphor literally in the most unexpected way. Original, character-perfect, and memorable.

jokes.text: Pheebs, this woman is voluntarily bald.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggests following advice from her bald friend
jokes.punchline: Rachel questions the credibility based on the baldness choice
jokes.explanation: Good logical point delivered efficiently. Rachel's practical skepticism comes through.

jokes.text: Or we can chant and dance around naked, you know, with sticks.
jokes.setup: Suggesting ritual alternatives to burning boyfriend items
jokes.punchline: Escalates to naked dancing with sticks
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe alternative lifestyle suggestion. The 'with sticks' detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You know... ever since I was little, I've been able to pick up quarters with my toes.
jokes.setup: Awkward first date conversation
jokes.punchline: Random, unsexy personal revelation
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward first date moment. Creates cringe but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Uh, quarters or rolls of quarters?
jokes.setup: Woman brags about picking up quarters with toes
jokes.punchline: Ross's technical follow-up question
jokes.explanation: Ross's scientific mind applied to absurd situation. Good deadpan response that shows his awkwardness.

jokes.text: By the way, Chandler, I cut you out of all of my pictures. So if you want, I have a bag with just your heads.
jokes.setup: Janice mentions cutting Chandler out of photos
jokes.punchline: Casually offers him a bag of severed photo heads
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific. Janice's matter-of-fact delivery of something creepy is perfect character work.

jokes.text: And you could use them in your Theater of Cruelty.
jokes.setup: Suggesting uses for the severed photo heads
jokes.punchline: References avant-garde theater concept for puppet show
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated reference that shows Janice's unexpected depth. Dark humor elevated by cultural knowledge.

jokes.text: She said she wants to slather my body with stuff and then lick it off. I'm not even sure what slathering is. But I definitely want to be a part of it.
jokes.setup: Joey explains why he has to leave dinner
jokes.punchline: His eager ignorance about sexual terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - enthusiasm overriding understanding. The admission of ignorance followed by eagerness is character-perfect.

jokes.text: I hope she throws up on you.
jokes.setup: Joey abandons Chandler with Janice
jokes.punchline: Chandler's spite disguised as well-wishes
jokes.explanation: Good spite joke. Chandler's bitterness expressed in crude but funny terms.

jokes.text: Although I have enjoyed the fact that your shirt's been sticking out of your zipper since you came back from the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Janice tries to find silver linings in bad date
jokes.punchline: Points out Chandler's wardrobe malfunction
jokes.explanation: Visual gag callback that adds embarrassment to earlier scene. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Joey's not a friend. He's... a stupid man who left us his credit card.
jokes.setup: Janice asks if they have the best friends
jokes.punchline: Chandler reframes Joey as a financial opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good reframe joke. Chandler's practical approach to dealing with abandonment shows character growth.

jokes.text: A bottle of your most overpriced champagne. - Each. - That's right. Each. Oh. And a, uh, Rob Roy. I've always wanted to know.
jokes.setup: Chandler decides to spend Joey's money
jokes.punchline: Escalates to expensive items plus random cocktail curiosity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific details. The Rob Roy curiosity adds character depth.

jokes.text: I'm just saying if dogs do experience jet lag, then... because of the whole seven-dog-years-to-one-human-year thing... that when a dog flies from New York to Los Angeles... he doesn't just lose three hours, he loses, like, a week and a half.
jokes.setup: Ross making conversation on his date
jokes.punchline: Overthinking dog jet lag with scientific precision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross - applying academic thinking to absurd scenarios. Original concept shows his character beautifully.

jokes.text: You mean they're lovers? If you want to put a label on it.
jokes.setup: Kristen asks about Ross's ex-wife and her friend
jokes.punchline: Ross's casual dismissal of the lesbian relationship
jokes.explanation: Ross's defensive minimization is perfectly in character. Good understatement that reveals his discomfort.

jokes.text: Oh, and she's pregnant with my baby. I always forget that part.
jokes.setup: Ross casually mentions his ex-wife's situation
jokes.punchline: Drops the pregnancy bomb as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and timing. Ross's casual delivery of major information is perfectly awkward and funny.

jokes.text: All I had is oregano and a Fresca.
jokes.setup: Need sage branches and sacramental wine for ritual
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical substitutions completely miss the point
jokes.explanation: Monica's practical approach to mystical requirements. Good contrast between spiritual and mundane.

jokes.text: All right. Now we need the semen of a righteous man.
jokes.setup: Continuing ritual ingredients
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact request for impossible ingredient
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's innocent delivery of inappropriate content. Creates awkwardness through her earnestness.

jokes.text: Huh. Okay, Pheebs, if we had that... we wouldn't be doing the ritual in the first place.
jokes.setup: Phoebe requests semen of righteous man
jokes.punchline: Rachel points out the logical contradiction
jokes.explanation: Excellent logical callback. Rachel's practical observation punctures the ritual's premise perfectly.

jokes.text: A picture of Scotty Jared, naked. - Let me see. He's wearing a sweater. - No.
jokes.setup: Monica claims to have naked photo
jokes.punchline: Photo shows man fully clothed in sweater
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with visual payoff. Simple but effective subversion of expectations.

jokes.text: Wait. Isn't it almost pure...? [ALL SCREAM]
jokes.setup: About to burn Paolo's grappa
jokes.punchline: Rachel realizes it's pure alcohol just as fire explodes
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy timing. Gets big reaction but not particularly clever setup.

jokes.text: Oh, man. In my next life I'm coming back as a toilet brush.
jokes.setup: Chandler's dating frustrations with Janice
jokes.punchline: Extreme self-deprecation about reincarnation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific self-deprecation. The toilet brush choice is unexpected and memorably pathetic.

jokes.text: I could just kiss you all over, and I'm gonna.
jokes.setup: Janice being affectionate with Joey
jokes.punchline: Her determination to follow through on uncomfortable promise
jokes.explanation: Janice's overwhelming affection creates discomfort. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: Kristen does something... that, funnily enough... wasn't even her major.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to introduce his date to his ex-wife
jokes.punchline: Repeats the woman's earlier awkward conversation
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Ross's desperation and social awkwardness. Situational comedy.

jokes.text: Wow, that guy's hairier than the chief.
jokes.setup: Fireman examining burned boyfriend items
jokes.punchline: Comments on hairiness in burned photo
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor from side character. Gets a chuckle but not memorable.

jokes.text: You're our third call tonight. Sure. Valentine's is our busiest night of the year.
jokes.setup: Girls embarrassed about boyfriend bonfire
jokes.punchline: Fireman reveals this is common on Valentine's
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Valentine's Day reality. Makes the girls' situation feel universal.

jokes.text: I brought you something. Is it loaded?
jokes.setup: Janice brings Chandler a gift
jokes.punchline: Chandler hopes it's a weapon
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and dark humor. Chandler's desperation expressed in two words is brilliant.

jokes.text: Oh, little candy hearts. 'Chan and Jan forever.' I had them made special.
jokes.setup: Janice's Valentine's gift to Chandler
jokes.punchline: Personalized candy hearts despite their relationship status
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Janice's oblivious romanticism creates discomfort perfectly.

jokes.text: You love me, Chandler Bing.
jokes.setup: Chandler tries to break up with Janice again
jokes.punchline: Janice declares his love despite his protests
jokes.explanation: Janice's delusional confidence is character-perfect. Sets up her longer speech.

jokes.text: Something deep in your soul calls out to me like a foghorn. Janice. Janice.
jokes.setup: Janice explaining why they keep getting together
jokes.punchline: Romantic metaphor becomes literal boat sound
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that becomes literal when she imitates the foghorn. Perfect Janice moment - romantic and ridiculous.

jokes.text: You know, this is still pretty hot.
jokes.setup: Ross's terrible date where woman left
jokes.punchline: His positive spin on obvious disaster
jokes.explanation: Ross's delusional optimism in face of clear rejection. Character-appropriate denial.

jokes.text: Let's say you and I give it another shot. - Ross. No, no, I know what you're gonna say. You're a lesbian.
jokes.setup: Ross suggests getting back together with his ex-wife
jokes.punchline: Dismisses her sexuality as minor obstacle
jokes.explanation: Ross's desperate logic and dismissal of fundamental incompatibility is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Let's just stick a pin in it, okay?
jokes.setup: Ross wants to ignore Carol's lesbianism
jokes.punchline: Treats sexual orientation like a meeting agenda item
jokes.explanation: Brilliant business metaphor applied to sexuality. Ross's academic approach to personal issues is perfect.

jokes.text: It's time to take the pin out.
jokes.setup: Carol ending the conversation with Ross
jokes.punchline: Uses Ross's own metaphor against him
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that turns Ross's metaphor against him. Great economy and structure.

jokes.text: All you need is a woman who likes men and you'll be set.
jokes.setup: Carol giving Ross dating advice
jokes.punchline: States the obvious requirement he's been missing
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Ross's situation. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I'll even let you ring the bell. Wohoo!
jokes.setup: Fireman inviting girls out
jokes.punchline: Rachel's enthusiastic response to bell-ringing offer
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo with predictable response. Functional but not clever.

jokes.text: You tell them you're married? - No way. My girlfriend doesn't know. I'm not gonna tell them.
jokes.setup: Firemen seeming like nice guys to the women
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're both cheating/lying
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of the 'nice guy' firemen. Dark ending that punctures romantic expectations.

tmdb_overview: Ross has a Valentine's date with a neighbor; Joey arranges a blind date for Chandler; others build a boyfriend bonfire.
